steps.title: Compare both table measures
steps.content: From 1910 to 1925, the table shows car production rising from 123,990 to 3,185,881. Over the same span, the number of companies falls from 320 to 80.

steps.title: Reject wrong trend pairs
steps.content: Choice B says both measures stayed unchanged. Choice C says production decreased. Choice D says the number of companies increased. The actual pattern is rising production with fewer companies. The answer is A.
